purpose: The product whitepaper / architectural narrative for Synapse — ~515 lines describing what Synapse is, why context preservation matters, why the developer must stay in the loop, and how every subsystem (Electron main process services, React frontend, SSE server, dispatch engine, dashboard, swarm builder, chat, IDE, git, live preview, PKI, multi-provider support) fits together. Reads as a long-form essay rather than a reference; intended for stakeholders, designers, and anyone trying to understand the philosophy and architecture without diving into code. Includes hand-drawn ASCII architecture diagrams that count exact file totals and line totals for each layer.
gotchas: ASCII counts in this file ('17 files, ~7000 lines', '25+ files, ~6000 lines', '12 files, ~2300 lines') are point-in-time snapshots from 2026-04-17 — they are NOT auto-updated and will drift as the codebase grows.

Calls out specific class/service names (SwarmOrchestrator, ClaudeCodeService, CodexService, PromptBuilder, etc.) — these correspond 1:1 to electron/services/* but renaming a service in code without updating this file produces silent doc rot.
Mentions Codex models 'GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4-Mini' — these are aspirational/forward-looking model names; verify against electron/services/CodexService.js before quoting.

States 'No database. No external service.' — this is true today, but the live preview webview and the optional GitHub auto-updater do hit the network, so the claim is about state storage, not network isolation.
Describes the SSE server as if it were a separate process; it actually runs inside Electron's main process via src/server/index.js (per package.json main = electron/main.js).
patterns: Architecture-as-narrative: each subsystem has a section that opens with a one-paragraph statement of purpose, then bullets the concrete capabilities — designers can read it top-to-bottom without code knowledge.
Pattern: every claim about agent behavior is anchored to a concrete file (e.g., 'see `electron/services/SwarmOrchestrator.js`') so the doc remains auditable.
Comparison framing: 'A conductor does not pick up a violin mid-symphony' / 'Air traffic controllers don't fly the planes' — recurring metaphors used to motivate the developer-in-the-loop philosophy.
conventions: Uses Unicode box-drawing characters (├──, └──) for architecture diagrams (unlike CLAUDE.md which uses ASCII).
Section dividers are `---` between every major topic.
All headings sentence-case after the H1 (`## How Context is Preserved and Optimized`).
